_note: Worked example: Cloudvane Systems, a fictional multi-tenant B2B SaaS vendor (seat + usage pricing). Demonstrates the SaaS realism musts: tenant size segments driving user counts and usage volume via scale_by, MRR quantized from plan price-per-seat x integer seats, a trial->active->dunning->churn subscription machine with trial-to-paid conversion visible in the data, invoices DERIVED from subscriptions + metered usage, a support triage machine, and entitlement/audit layers. Keys starting with '_' are comments and are stripped before validation.

calendar._note: B2B weekly shape with a Monday support peak and a quiet weekend; January/September signup waves and a December trough; 18% YoY growth.

tables.name: application
tables.purpose: Application catalog for the fictional landscape.

tables._note: Every signup ever, including dead trials. created_at is sorted with 55% pre-window backfill: tenant IDs correlate with signup order and the paying book is tenured rather than ramping from zero. Lifecycle truth lives in the subscription machine; core.tenant derives a status FROM subscription states so the two can never disagree.
tables.name: tenant
tables.purpose: Tenant master in the product app database: one row per organization that ever signed up.

columns.name: segment
columns._note: Drawn first: every volume and dollar lever downstream conditions on it.

columns.name: contact_email
columns._note: Role addresses on the tenant's own fictional example.com subdomain.

columns.name: created_at
columns._note: sorted + backfill: IDs correlate with signup dates; 55% of tenants predate the window (company founded 2021).

tables.name: feature
tables.purpose: Product feature catalog with plan-rank gating and metered flags.

tables.name: workspace
tables.purpose: Workspaces created inside each tenant.

columns.name: workspace_name
columns._note: Real workspace names repeat across tenants ('Marketing', 'Sales Ops') — unique names everywhere would be a tell.

tables._note: Identity provider users, 1+ per tenant. Volume scales with the tenant's size segment (enterprise ~8x smb), which is what makes the seat true-up control meaningful.
tables.name: user
tables.purpose: IdP user directory: provisioned identities per tenant.

columns.name: status
columns._note: 12% deactivated but still provisioned — feeds the seat true-up control.

tables.name: sso_connection
tables.purpose: SAML/OIDC connections; concentrated in enterprise tenants.

tables._note: The big event table. business_hours:false gives the automated 7-day feel (SDKs, scheduled jobs, global users) while weekday_weights still shape weekly volume. scale_by segment + heavy-tailed per_parent_multiplier produce usage whales: top 5% of tenants carry ~half the event volume. user_id has a deliberate 0.4% cross-tenant leak that the tenant-isolation DQ rule (DQ-008) catches.
tables.name: usage_event
tables.purpose: Product usage events from the metering pipeline.
tables.grain: one row per product usage event

columns.name: user_id
columns._note: fk affinity match on tenant keeps tenant isolation; leak_rate 0.004 is the deliberate isolation defect for DQ-008.

columns.name: feature_id
columns._note: zipf default 1.1: the core Dashboards feature carries ~a third of events, which is how real product telemetry looks.

columns.name: units
columns._note: Metered units carried by the event (API calls / rows in the batch) — calibrated so ~10-20% of tenant-months exceed their plan allowance and bill overage.

tables.name: plan
tables.purpose: Published price book: per-seat price, included metered units, overage rate, and the segment each plan targets.

columns.name: segment_target
columns._note: fk affinity matching key: subscriptions pick a plan whose target matches their tenant segment (4% leak = sales exceptions).

columns.name: price_per_seat
columns._note: Published round price points — MRR everywhere downstream is quantized to seats x these.

columns.name: included_units
columns._note: Monthly metering-credit allowance, anchored near the p85 of each plan's observed tenant-month units so ~10-15% of invoices bill overage, concentrated in the smaller plans (upgrade pressure).

tables._note: Billing platform's own account record, generated 1:1 per tenant with parent_copy so the two systems agree until imperfections make them disagree (late feeds, staging conflicts, missing xref).
tables.name: customer_account
tables.purpose: Billing platform customer account, fed from the product app but maintained separately.

tables._note: Every subscription enters the machine at 'trialing'; 28% convert to active (trial-to-paid is visible in the data: activated_at NULL for the 72% that expired). MRR = seats x plan price_per_seat via fk_copy + expr, so amounts are quantized to the price book. ~12% of tenants have a second subscription episode (re-signup after churn).
tables.name: subscription
tables.purpose: Subscription episodes per tenant; lifecycle driven by the subscription_lifecycle machine.
tables.grain: one row per tenant per subscription episode

columns.name: plan_id
columns._note: Affinity match: plan targets the tenant's segment, 4% leak = sales exceptions on the wrong plan.

columns.name: mrr_amount
columns._note: Quantized: integer seats x published per-seat price.

columns.name: started_at
columns._note: Trial starts hours after tenant signup (short process lag, the legit date_offset use).

columns.name: comp_flag
columns._note: manual_override imperfection sets this to 1 on ~2%: comped by sales but still billed full rate -> DQ-011.

tables.name: subscription_event
tables.purpose: Subscription status history emitted by the lifecycle machine.
tables.grain: one row per subscription per state entered

tables._note: Everything from here in the bill schema is DERIVED from generated tables: metering rolls up usage events, invoices roll up subscriptions + metering, lines explode invoices, dunning schedules derive from machine timestamps. Real lineage, not parallel-faked data.
tables.name: meter_reading
tables.purpose: Monthly metered-unit rollup per tenant, summed from usage events on metered features.
tables.grain: one row per tenant per calendar month with metered usage

tables.name: invoice
tables.purpose: Monthly invoices derived from converted subscriptions + metered overage; ~60% bill on the 1st, the rest on anniversary days.
tables.grain: one row per converted subscription per active calendar month

tables.name: invoice_line
tables.purpose: Invoice lines: subscription fee, metered overage, occasional quarter-end credit.
tables.grain: one row per invoice per line number

tables.name: dunning_attempt
tables.purpose: Card retry schedule derived from dunning episodes: +3/+7/+14 day retries and a +20 day final notice.
tables.grain: one row per dunning episode per retry attempt

tables.name: entitlement
tables.purpose: Feature entitlements derived from live subscriptions x the plan's feature set, plus explicit overrides; stale_mapping leaves 2% expired-but-active for DQ-007.
tables.grain: one row per live subscription per entitled feature

tables._note: Tickets carry a free-text tenant name (tenant_name_entered) the way real helpdesks do; the typo imperfection corrupts 5% so ticket-to-tenant matching needs the fuzzy-join story (DQ-009). requester orphans (3%) model requesters missing from the IdP after offboarding (DQ-002).
tables.name: ticket
tables.purpose: Support tickets; triage lifecycle driven by the ticket_triage machine.

columns.name: requester_user_id
columns._note: Same-tenant requester (leak 0); orphan_fk later ghosts 3% (offboarded users).

tables.name: ticket_event
tables.purpose: Ticket status history emitted by the triage machine.
tables.grain: one row per ticket per state entered

tables.name: csat_response
tables.purpose: CSAT surveys: 22% of resolved tickets respond; escalated tickets score ~a point lower.
tables.grain: one row per responding resolved ticket

tables.name: change_event
tables.purpose: Field-level change history on billing subscriptions (seat changes, plan moves, billing detail edits).

columns.name: old_value
columns._note: Old/new values stay plausible for the changed field.

tables.name: webhook_event
tables.purpose: Payment processor webhook deliveries landed by the integration bus; retries duplicate ~1.2% (DQ-005 dedupes by event_uid).
tables.grain: one row per webhook delivery received

tables.name: sku_revenue_line_mapping
tables.purpose: Revenue-operations spreadsheet mapping billing SKUs to finance revenue lines; 7 of 8 live invoice SKUs are mapped (87.5% coverage), with stale and conflicting rows retained.

tables.name: job
tables.purpose: Scheduled data-platform jobs paired to cataloged code objects and outbound extracts.

tables.name: job_run
tables.purpose: Run history for scheduled data-platform jobs; deterministic failed and partial runs create realistic operations history.

cases.failed: Upstream file arrived after watermark and retry budget was exhausted.
cases.partial: Late-arriving records held for the next scheduled run.

tables.name: rule
tables.purpose: Data quality rule catalog (results come from the dq_rule_result_current view).

cases.2: ticket requester not found in IdP
cases.10: staging account name disagrees with source
cases.11: comped subscription billed at full rate
cases.12: invoice SKU missing finance revenue-line mapping
cases.13: replayed metering event deduped in staging

tables.name: control
tables.purpose: Reconciliation control catalog; break populations live in the control_* views.

cases.1: Invoice header total ties to invoice lines
cases.2: Provisioned IdP users vs billed seats true-up
cases.4: Finance ARR ties to customer-success ARR definition
cases.5: Metered usage staging ties to monthly rated billing

tables._note: raw -> stg -> xref -> core flow, all SQL-derived from bill.customer_account / app.tenant so lineage (and propagated defects) are real.
tables.name: billing_account_extract
tables.purpose: Nightly billing platform account extract as landed: all text, US-format dates, file lineage, batch ids, and ingest timestamps.

tables.name: billing_account
tables.purpose: Cleaned billing accounts; normalizes raw extract dates (format drift parses to NULL and DQ-004 catches it).

tables.name: idp_tenant_export
tables.purpose: Identity-provider tenant export landed as text with file ids, batch ids, and ingest timestamp.

tables.name: idp_tenant
tables.purpose: Typed IdP tenant staging with guarded date parsing.

tables.name: idp_user_export
tables.purpose: Identity-provider user export landed as text with file ids, batch ids, and ingest timestamp.

tables.name: idp_user
tables.purpose: Typed IdP user staging with guarded timestamp parsing.

tables.name: usage_metering_event
tables.purpose: Raw metering events from product telemetry, including replayed deliveries before staging deduplication.

tables.name: usage_metering_event
tables.purpose: Deduplicated metering event staging; replayed raw events collapse to the latest ingested event_uid.

tables.name: billing_invoice_extract
tables.purpose: Billing invoice extract as landed from the billing platform with file/batch lineage and text amounts.

tables.name: billing_invoice
tables.purpose: Typed billing invoice staging with guarded invoice and due-date parsing.

tables.name: support_ticket_export
tables.purpose: Support ticket export landed as text timestamps with file and batch lineage.

tables.name: support_ticket
tables.purpose: Typed support ticket staging with guarded timestamp parsing.

tables.name: entity_identifier
tables.purpose: Source-system identifier crosswalk for canonical tenants (app platform + billing platform).

tables.name: tenant
tables.purpose: Canonical tenant joining the app platform and billing platform through the crosswalk; lifecycle_status is DERIVED from subscription machine states so status and timestamps can never disagree.

tables.name: dim_date
tables.purpose: Calendar dimension.

tables.name: dim_tenant
tables.purpose: Tenant dimension from the canonical layer.

tables.name: dim_plan
tables.purpose: Plan dimension from the billing price book.

tables.name: dim_feature
tables.purpose: Feature dimension.

tables.name: fact_invoice_line
tables.purpose: Invoice line fact.

tables.name: fact_subscription_month
tables.purpose: Semi-additive MRR snapshot fact.
tables.grain: one row per converted subscription per active calendar month

tables.name: fact_usage_daily
tables.purpose: Daily metered usage fact built from deduplicated staging events.
tables.grain: one row per tenant, feature, and event date

tables.name: fact_support_ticket
tables.purpose: Accumulating-snapshot ticket fact with SLA targets and breach flags.

tables.name: month_end_arr_snapshot
tables.purpose: Materialized month-end ARR snapshot rebuilt by the cataloged refresh procedure.
tables.grain: one row per period month and tenant segment

tables.name: mapping_request_case
tables.purpose: Revenue-operations workflow cases opened for invoice SKUs that lack an active finance mapping.

tables.name: code_object
tables.purpose: Catalog of deployed views plus stored procedure, function, and extract definitions used by the data platform.

tables.name: lineage_edge
tables.purpose: Dataset and code lineage edges for the warehouse/view stack, jobs, and outbound extracts.

state_machines._note: Trial-to-paid 0.28 (trialing->active); voluntary churn and dunning exhaustion both visible. Dunning recovery lands in 'recovered' (not back to 'active') so activated_at is never overwritten and invoice windows stay anchored to first conversion.
state_machines.name: subscription_lifecycle

state_machines._note: Triage machine: ~9% escalate, 6% reopen, waiting_customer ping-pong, right-censored so recent tickets legitimately sit open.
state_machines.name: ticket_triage

derivations.name: raw_billing_account_extract
derivations._note: Raw landing copy with US-format dates; format_drift later pads a slice, stg parsing turns those into NULLs, DQ-004 catches them.
sql: select cast(account_id as text), account_code, cast(tenant_id as text), account_name, segment,

derivations.name: stg_billing_account
sql: select cast(account_id as integer), account_code, cast(tenant_ref as integer), trim(account_name),

derivations.name: raw_idp_tenant_export
sql: select row_number() over (order by t.tenant_id), cast(t.tenant_id as text), t.tenant_name, t.segment, t.region,
cast(sum(case when u.status = 'active' then 1 else 0 end) as text),
from app.tenant t left join idp.user u on u.tenant_id = t.tenant_id
group by t.tenant_id, t.tenant_name, t.segment, t.region, t.created_at;

derivations.name: stg_idp_tenant
sql: select cast(tenant_ref as integer), trim(tenant_name), lower(segment), lower(region),
then substr(exported_at_text,7,4) || '-' || substr(exported_at_text,1,2) || '-' || substr(exported_at_text,4,2) || substr(exported_at_text,11) end,

derivations.name: raw_idp_user_export
sql: select row_number() over (order by u.user_id), cast(u.user_id as text), cast(u.tenant_id as text),
u.email, u.idp_role, u.status, cast(u.mfa_enabled as text),
case when u.last_login_at is not null then strftime('%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S', u.last_login_at) end,

derivations.name: stg_idp_user
sql: select cast(user_ref as integer), cast(tenant_ref as integer), lower(trim(email)), idp_role, status,
case when mfa_enabled_text in ('1', 'true', 'True') then 1 else 0 end,
then substr(created_at_text,7,4) || '-' || substr(created_at_text,1,2) || '-' || substr(created_at_text,4,2) || substr(created_at_text,11) end,
then substr(last_login_at_text,7,4) || '-' || substr(last_login_at_text,1,2) || '-' || substr(last_login_at_text,4,2) || substr(last_login_at_text,11) end,

derivations.name: raw_usage_metering_event
derivations._note: Adds deterministic replay deliveries for about 1% of usage events; staging keeps the latest raw row per event_uid.
sql: select e.event_id, 'uev-' || printf('%09d', e.event_id), cast(e.tenant_id as text), coalesce(cast(e.user_id as text), ''),
cast(e.feature_id as text), e.event_type, cast(e.units as text), e.client,
select 900000000 + e.event_id, 'uev-' || printf('%09d', e.event_id), cast(e.tenant_id as text), coalesce(cast(e.user_id as text), ''),
cast(e.feature_id as text), e.event_type, cast(e.units as text), e.client,

derivations.name: stg_usage_metering_event
sql: row_number() over (partition by event_uid order by ingested_at desc, raw_event_row_id desc) as rn,
count(*) over (partition by event_uid) as replay_count
select event_uid, cast(substr(event_uid, 5) as integer), cast(tenant_ref as integer),
case when user_ref = '' then null else cast(user_ref as integer) end,
cast(feature_ref as integer), event_type, cast(units_text as integer), client,
then substr(event_at_text,7,4) || '-' || substr(event_at_text,1,2) || '-' || substr(event_at_text,4,2) || substr(event_at_text,11) end,
then substr(sdk_received_at_text,7,4) || '-' || substr(sdk_received_at_text,1,2) || '-' || substr(sdk_received_at_text,4,2) || substr(sdk_received_at_text,11) end,

derivations.name: xref_entity_identifier
derivations._note: Both systems mapped per canonical tenant; missing_xref deletes 3% so crosswalk coverage is realistically <100%.
sql: select row_number() over (order by t.tenant_id), 'tenant', t.tenant_id,
'Atriumly App Platform', 'tenant', cast(t.tenant_id as text), date(t.created_at), null, 1
select 500000 + row_number() over (order by a.account_id), 'tenant', a.tenant_id,
'LedgerPilot Billing', 'customer_account', cast(a.account_id as text), date(a.created_at), null, 1

derivations.name: core_tenant
derivations._note: lifecycle_status derived FROM subscription machine states — status-vs-timestamp coherence by construction.
sql: (select cast(x.source_record_id as integer) from xref.entity_identifier x
where x.source_system = 'LedgerPilot Billing' and x.canonical_entity_id = t.tenant_id
case when exists (select 1 from bill.subscription s where s.tenant_id = t.tenant_id
when exists (select 1 from bill.subscription s where s.tenant_id = t.tenant_id
when exists (select 1 from bill.subscription s where s.tenant_id = t.tenant_id

derivations.name: bill_meter_reading
derivations._note: Sums only metered features. Orphaned feature events (retired features) drop out of the join — metering silently undercounts, exactly the symptom the meter-to-bill recon would chase.
sql: select row_number() over (order by e.tenant_id, strftime('%Y-%m', e.event_at)),
join app.feature f on f.feature_id = e.feature_id and f.is_metered = 1

derivations.name: bill_invoice
derivations._note: Month spine x converted subscriptions: only subscriptions that reached 'active' bill (trial-to-paid visible in billing). ~60% calendar-aligned to the 1st, the rest anniversary-dated; due dates honor card autopay vs invoiced terms; status logic ties payment failure to the dunning month from the machine.
sql: union all select date(month_start, '+1 month') from months where month_start < date('2025-12-01')
select s.subscription_id, s.tenant_id, s.segment, s.plan_id, s.seats, s.mrr_amount,
strftime('%Y-%m', m.month_start) as period_month, date(s.activated_at) as activation_date
else date(e.month_start, '+' || (e.subscription_id % 26 + 1) || ' days') end as invoice_date
join bill.plan p on p.plan_id = e.plan_id
join bill.customer_account ca on ca.tenant_id = e.tenant_id
left join bill.meter_reading mr on mr.tenant_id = e.tenant_id and mr.period_month = e.period_month
else date(p.invoice_date, '+' || case p.payment_terms when 'net45' then 45
when 'net30' then 30 when 'net15' then 15 else 0 end || ' days') end as due_date,
case when (p.subscription_id + cast(strftime('%m', p.month_start) as integer)) % 37 = 0
select row_number() over (order by a.subscription_id, a.month_start),
'INV-' || printf('%07d', row_number() over (order by a.subscription_id, a.month_start)),
a.subscription_id, a.tenant_id, a.segment, a.period_month, a.invoice_date, a.due_date,
when a.dunning_started_at is not null and a.churned_at is not null
when (a.subscription_id * 7 + cast(strftime('%m', a.month_start) as integer)) % 10 < 8 then 'paid'
and (a.subscription_id * 7 + cast(strftime('%m', a.month_start) as integer)) % 10 = 9 then 'written_off'

derivations.name: raw_billing_invoice_extract
sql: select row_number() over (order by i.invoice_id), cast(i.invoice_id as text), i.invoice_number,
cast(i.subscription_id as text), cast(i.tenant_id as text), i.period_month,
printf('%.2f', i.base_amount), cast(i.overage_units as text), printf('%.2f', i.overage_amount),

derivations.name: stg_billing_invoice
sql: select cast(invoice_ref as integer), invoice_number, cast(subscription_ref as integer), cast(tenant_ref as integer), period_month,
cast(base_amount_text as real), cast(overage_units_text as integer), cast(overage_amount_text as real),
cast(credit_amount_text as real), cast(invoice_total_text as real), status, landing_file_id, batch_id, ingested_at

derivations.name: bill_invoice_line
derivations._note: invoice_line_id = invoice_id*10 + line_number keeps IDs deterministic and clear of the imperfection injectors' synthetic ranges. Line amounts tie exactly to the header until restatement_reversal breaks ~1.2% for CTL-001.
sql: p.plan_name || ' plan - ' || s.seats || ' seats', s.seats, s.price_per_seat,
join bill.subscription s on s.subscription_id = i.subscription_id
join bill.plan p on p.plan_id = s.plan_id;
'Metered usage overage - ' || i.overage_units || ' units over plan', 1,

derivations.name: bill_dunning_attempt
derivations._note: Retry echo at +3/+7/+14/+20 days from the machine's dunning_started_at; churned episodes run the full schedule to final notice, recovered ones stop at the recovering attempt, open episodes show attempts up to as_of.
sql: when s.churned_at is not null then
case when sched.attempt_number = 4 then 'final_notice' else 'failed' end
when s.recovered_at is not null and s.recovered_at > s.dunning_started_at then
case when sched.attempt_number = 1 + (s.subscription_id % 3) then 'recovered' else 'failed' end
case when sched.attempt_number = 4 then 'final_notice_email' else 'card_retry' end
or (s.churned_at is null and s.recovered_at is not null and s.recovered_at > s.dunning_started_at
or (s.churned_at is null and (s.recovered_at is null or s.recovered_at <= s.dunning_started_at))

derivations.name: bill_entitlement
derivations._note: Live subscriptions x plan feature set (plan_rank >= min_plan_rank) plus ~4% explicit overrides one rank above plan — the override rows are the ones an entitlement audit asks about.
sql: select row_number() over (order by s.subscription_id, f.feature_id),
case when f.min_plan_rank <= p.plan_rank then 'plan' else 'override' end
join bill.plan p on p.plan_id = s.plan_id

derivations.name: raw_support_ticket_export
sql: select row_number() over (order by t.ticket_id), cast(t.ticket_id as text), t.ticket_number, cast(t.tenant_id as text),
t.tenant_name_entered, coalesce(cast(t.requester_user_id as text), ''), t.priority, t.channel, t.product_area, t.status,
case when t.first_response_at is not null then strftime('%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S', t.first_response_at) end,
case when t.resolved_at is not null then strftime('%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S', t.resolved_at) end,
case when t.closed_at is not null then strftime('%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S', t.closed_at) end,

derivations.name: stg_support_ticket
sql: select cast(ticket_ref as integer), ticket_number, cast(tenant_ref as integer), trim(tenant_name_entered),
case when requester_ref = '' then null else cast(requester_ref as integer) end,
then substr(opened_at_text,7,4) || '-' || substr(opened_at_text,1,2) || '-' || substr(opened_at_text,4,2) || substr(opened_at_text,11) end,
then substr(first_response_at_text,7,4) || '-' || substr(first_response_at_text,1,2) || '-' || substr(first_response_at_text,4,2) || substr(first_response_at_text,11) end,
then substr(resolved_at_text,7,4) || '-' || substr(resolved_at_text,1,2) || '-' || substr(resolved_at_text,4,2) || substr(resolved_at_text,11) end,
then substr(closed_at_text,7,4) || '-' || substr(closed_at_text,1,2) || '-' || substr(closed_at_text,4,2) || substr(closed_at_text,11) end,

derivations.name: sup_csat_response
derivations._note: 22% response rate (ticket_id % 9 < 2); escalated tickets score ~a point lower; detractor comments only on low scores.
sql: case when exists (select 1 from sup.ticket_event e where e.ticket_id = t.ticket_id and e.state = 'escalated')
then case t.ticket_id % 8 when 0 then 1 when 1 then 2 when 2 then 2 when 3 then 3
when 4 then 3 when 5 then 4 when 6 then 4 else 5 end
else case when t.ticket_id % 20 = 0 then 1 when t.ticket_id % 20 = 1 then 2
when t.ticket_id % 20 in (2, 3) then 3 when t.ticket_id % 20 in (4, 5, 6, 7, 8) then 4
case when t.ticket_id % 20 = 0 then 'Took far too long and we had to chase for updates.'
when t.ticket_id % 20 = 1 then 'Issue came back after it was marked resolved.'
when t.ticket_id % 3 = 0 then 'Fast and helpful, thanks.'
where t.resolved_at is not null and t.ticket_id % 9 < 2

derivations.name: wh_dim_date
sql: union all select date(dt, '+1 day') from d where dt < '2025-12-31'
select dt, cast(strftime('%Y', dt) as integer), cast(strftime('%m', dt) as integer),
cast(strftime('%d', dt) as integer), cast(strftime('%w', dt) as integer),
case when strftime('%w', dt) in ('0', '6') then 1 else 0 end

derivations.name: wh_dim_tenant
sql: select tenant_uid, tenant_name, segment, region, lifecycle_status, signup_date from core.tenant;

derivations.name: wh_dim_plan
sql: select plan_id, plan_code, plan_name, segment_target, plan_rank, price_per_seat from bill.plan;

derivations.name: wh_dim_feature
sql: select feature_id, feature_code, feature_name, product_area, min_plan_rank, is_metered from app.feature;

derivations.name: wh_fact_invoice_line
sql: select l.invoice_line_id, l.invoice_id, i.period_month, i.invoice_date, i.tenant_id, i.segment,
from bill.invoice_line l join bill.invoice i on i.invoice_id = l.invoice_id;

derivations.name: wh_fact_subscription_month
sql: select i.invoice_id, i.period_month, i.subscription_id, i.tenant_id, i.segment,
join bill.subscription s on s.subscription_id = i.subscription_id
join bill.plan p on p.plan_id = s.plan_id;

derivations.name: wh_fact_usage_daily
sql: select row_number() over (order by date(event_at), tenant_id, feature_id),
group by date(event_at), strftime('%Y-%m', event_at), tenant_id, feature_id;

derivations.name: wh_fact_support_ticket
sql: select t.ticket_id, t.tenant_id, a.segment, t.priority, t.channel, t.product_area,
case t.priority when 'urgent' then 60 when 'high' then 240 when 'normal' then 480 else 1440 end,
case when t.first_response_at is not null
case when t.first_response_at is not null
(case t.priority when 'urgent' then 60 when 'high' then 240 when 'normal' then 480 else 1440 end)
case when t.resolved_at is not null
join app.tenant a on a.tenant_id = t.tenant_id;

derivations.name: nv_subscription_month
sql: f.subscription_id, f.tenant_id, t.tenant_name, t.segment, t.region, t.lifecycle_status,
p.plan_key, p.plan_code, p.plan_name, p.segment_target, f.seats, f.mrr_amount,
join wh.dim_tenant t on t.tenant_key = f.tenant_id
join wh.dim_plan p on p.plan_code = f.plan_code
left join wh.dim_date d on d.date_key = f.period_month || '-01';

derivations.name: nv_invoice_line
sql: select f.invoice_line_id, f.invoice_id, i.invoice_number, f.period_month, f.invoice_date,
d.year_num, d.month_num, f.tenant_id, t.tenant_name, t.segment, t.region,
when f.item_type = 'service_credit' then 'SERVICE_CREDIT' else upper(f.item_type) end as source_sku_code,
join bill.invoice i on i.invoice_id = f.invoice_id
join bill.subscription s on s.subscription_id = i.subscription_id
join wh.dim_plan p on p.plan_key = s.plan_id
join wh.dim_tenant t on t.tenant_key = f.tenant_id
left join wh.dim_date d on d.date_key = f.invoice_date;

derivations.name: nv_usage_daily
sql: select f.usage_daily_id, f.usage_date, f.period_month, d.year_num, d.month_num,
f.tenant_id, t.tenant_name, t.segment, t.region, f.feature_id, fe.feature_code,
join wh.dim_tenant t on t.tenant_key = f.tenant_id
left join wh.dim_feature fe on fe.feature_key = f.feature_id
left join wh.dim_date d on d.date_key = f.usage_date;

derivations.name: bv_mrr_movements
sql: select period_month, segment, count(distinct subscription_id) as active_subscriptions,
round(sum(mrr_amount), 2) as ending_mrr, round(sum(overage_amount), 2) as overage_billed
from nv_subscription_month group by period_month, segment
select monthly.*, lag(ending_mrr) over (partition by segment order by period_month) as opening_mrr
select period_month, segment, active_subscriptions, coalesce(opening_mrr, 0) as opening_mrr,
case when opening_mrr is null then ending_mrr when ending_mrr > opening_mrr then round(ending_mrr - opening_mrr, 2) else 0 end as new_expansion_mrr,
case when opening_mrr is not null and ending_mrr < opening_mrr then round(opening_mrr - ending_mrr, 2) else 0 end as contraction_churn_mrr,

derivations.name: bv_usage_by_tenant
sql: select u.period_month, u.tenant_id, u.tenant_name, u.segment, u.region,
sum(u.metered_units) as metered_units, sum(u.event_count) as event_count,
max(u.active_users) as active_users, sum(u.replayed_raw_events) as replayed_raw_events,
left join nv_subscription_month s on s.tenant_id = u.tenant_id and s.period_month = u.period_month
group by u.period_month, u.tenant_id, u.tenant_name, u.segment, u.region;

derivations.name: bv_trial_funnel
sql: select strftime('%Y-%m', s.started_at) as trial_month, t.segment, t.signup_channel,
sum(case when s.activated_at is not null then 1 else 0 end) as trials_converted,
sum(case when s.status = 'expired_trial' then 1 else 0 end) as trials_expired,
round(1.0 * sum(case when s.activated_at is not null then 1 else 0 end) / count(*), 3) as conversion_rate,
round(avg(case when s.activated_at is not null then julianday(s.activated_at) - julianday(s.started_at) end), 1) as avg_days_to_convert
from bill.subscription s join app.tenant t on t.tenant_id = s.tenant_id
group by strftime('%Y-%m', s.started_at), t.segment, t.signup_channel;

derivations.name: mv_month_end_arr_snapshot
sql: select row_number() over (order by period_month, segment), period_month, segment, active_subscriptions,

derivations.name: mart_finance_arr_monthly
derivations._note: Finance applies the manual SKU revenue-line mapping; customer-success intentionally does not, creating the controlled ARR discrepancy.
sql: select m.*, row_number() over (partition by source_sku_code order by approved_flag desc, current_flag desc, mapping_priority desc, effective_start_date desc, mapping_id desc) as rn
select n.period_month, n.segment, coalesce(m.finance_revenue_line, 'UNMAPPED') as finance_revenue_line,
round(sum(case when m.arr_treatment in ('recurring', 'metered') then n.line_amount * 12 else 0 end), 2) as finance_arr,
sum(case when m.source_sku_code is null then 1 else 0 end) as unmapped_line_count
left join active_mapping m on m.source_sku_code = n.source_sku_code
and n.invoice_date between m.effective_start_date and coalesce(m.effective_end_date, '9999-12-31')

derivations.name: mart_customersuccess_arr_monthly
sql: select m.period_month, m.segment, m.active_subscriptions, m.ending_arr as customer_success_arr,
coalesce(u.active_tenants, 0) as active_usage_tenants, coalesce(u.metered_units, 0) as metered_units,
case when m.contraction_churn_mrr > 0 then 'watch' when coalesce(u.metered_units, 0) = 0 then 'low_usage' else 'healthy' end as cs_arr_status
select period_month, segment, count(distinct tenant_id) as active_tenants, sum(metered_units) as metered_units
from bv_usage_by_tenant group by period_month, segment
) u on u.period_month = m.period_month and u.segment = m.segment;

derivations.name: mart_mrr_monthly
sql: select period_month, segment, active_subscriptions as subscriptions, ending_mrr as mrr,

derivations.name: mart_feature_adoption_monthly
sql: select period_month, product_area, feature_name, sum(event_count) as events,
sum(metered_units) as units, count(distinct tenant_id) as active_tenants

derivations.name: control_recon_invoice_lines
derivations._note: Reconciliation as a VIEW so it reflects post-derivation restatement reversals on invoice lines (CTL-001 breaks).
sql: join bill.invoice_line l on l.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
group by i.invoice_id, i.invoice_number, i.period_month, i.invoice_total

derivations.name: control_seat_trueup
derivations._note: Seat license true-up (CTL-002): live subscriptions where provisioned active IdP users drift from billed seats by more than max(2, 5%) — lagging deprovisioning and under-licensing both surface.
sql: select s.subscription_id, s.tenant_id, t.tenant_name, s.segment, s.seats as billed_seats,
join app.tenant t on t.tenant_id = s.tenant_id
join (select tenant_id, count(*) as provisioned_users from stg.idp_user
where status = 'active' group by tenant_id) u on u.tenant_id = s.tenant_id

derivations.name: control_recon_arr_finance_vs_cs
sql: select period_month, segment, round(sum(finance_arr), 2) as finance_arr, sum(unmapped_line_count) as unmapped_line_count
from mart_finance_arr_monthly group by period_month, segment
select period_month, segment, customer_success_arr from mart_customersuccess_arr_monthly
select coalesce(f.period_month, cs.period_month) as period_month, coalesce(f.segment, cs.segment) as segment,
coalesce(f.finance_arr, 0) as finance_arr, coalesce(cs.customer_success_arr, 0) as customer_success_arr,
from finance f join cs on cs.period_month = f.period_month and cs.segment = f.segment

derivations.name: control_recon_metered_billing
sql: select tenant_id, strftime('%Y-%m', event_at) as period_month, sum(units) as staged_units, sum(replay_count - 1) as replayed_event_rows
from stg.usage_metering_event where event_at is not null group by tenant_id, period_month
select tenant_id, period_month, sum(metered_units) as rated_units from bill.meter_reading group by tenant_id, period_month
select s.tenant_id, s.period_month, s.staged_units, coalesce(r.rated_units, 0) as rated_units,
from staged s left join rated r on r.tenant_id = s.tenant_id and r.period_month = s.period_month

derivations.name: workflow_mapping_request_case
sql: select n.source_sku_code, n.period_month, count(*) as line_count, round(sum(n.line_amount), 2) as amount
where not exists (select 1 from active_mapping m where m.source_sku_code = n.source_sku_code)
select row_number() over (order by source_sku_code, period_month), 'SKU-MAP-' || printf('%05d', row_number() over (order by source_sku_code, period_month)),
case when abs(amount) > 10000 then 'high' else 'normal' end, 'revenue_ops_mapping',

derivations.name: dq_rule_result_current
sql: select 'DQ-001' as rule_code, 'billing account missing billing email' as issue, cast(account_id as text) as entity_id
from bill.customer_account where billing_email is null or billing_email = ''
select 'DQ-002', 'ticket requester not found in IdP', cast(t.ticket_id as text)
and not exists (select 1 from idp.user u where u.user_id = t.requester_user_id)
select 'DQ-003', 'tenant missing billing crosswalk mapping', cast(t.tenant_id as text)
where not exists (select 1 from xref.entity_identifier x
select 'DQ-004', 'unparseable raw billing account extract date', account_id
from raw.billing_account_extract where source_created_date not glob '[0-9][0-9]/[0-9][0-9]/[0-9][0-9][0-9][0-9]'
from integration.webhook_event group by event_uid having count(*) > 1
select 'DQ-006', 'usage event references retired feature', cast(e.event_id as text)
where not exists (select 1 from app.feature f where f.feature_id = e.feature_id)
select 'DQ-007', 'entitlement past valid_to still active', cast(entitlement_id as text)
from bill.entitlement where active_flag = 1 and valid_to is not null and valid_to < '2025-12-31'
select 'DQ-008', 'cross-tenant usage event (isolation breach)', cast(e.event_id as text)
from app.usage_event e join idp.user u on u.user_id = e.user_id
select 'DQ-009', 'ticket free-text tenant name mismatch', cast(t.ticket_id as text)
from sup.ticket t join app.tenant a on a.tenant_id = t.tenant_id
select 'DQ-010', 'staging account name disagrees with source', cast(sb.account_id as text)
from stg.billing_account sb join bill.customer_account ca on ca.account_id = sb.account_id
select 'DQ-011', 'comped subscription billed at full rate', cast(s.subscription_id as text)
and exists (select 1 from bill.invoice i where i.subscription_id = s.subscription_id and i.base_amount > 0)
select 'DQ-012', 'invoice line source SKU missing finance revenue mapping', source_sku_code || ':' || period_month
where not exists (select 1 from manual.sku_revenue_line_mapping m
where m.source_sku_code = n.source_sku_code and m.approved_flag = 1 and m.current_flag = 1)
select 'DQ-013', 'replayed metering event deduped in staging', event_uid
from raw.usage_metering_event group by event_uid having count(*) > 1
select 'DQ-014', 'unparseable billing invoice extract date', invoice_ref
from raw.billing_invoice_extract where invoice_date_text not glob '[0-9][0-9]/[0-9][0-9]/[0-9][0-9][0-9][0-9]'

derivations.name: catalog_code_object
derivations._note: Views self-register from sqlite_master, then stored procedure/function/extract definitions are cataloged as source text.
sql: select row_number() over (order by name), name, 'view', 'sqlite', sql, 'data_platform',
where type = 'view' and name not like 'sqlite_%';

'create procedure sp_rate_monthly_metered_billing(p_period_month text) as insert into bill.meter_reading select row_number() over (order by tenant_id), tenant_id, p_period_month, sum(units), count(*) from stg.usage_metering_event where strftime(''%Y-%m'', event_at) = p_period_month group by tenant_id;',
'billing_ops', '2025-02-01 10:00:00', '2025-12-02 18:20:00', 'deployed', 'Monthly metered-billing rating routine stored as catalog source text';

'create procedure sp_rebuild_mv_month_end_arr_snapshot() as delete from mv.month_end_arr_snapshot; insert into mv.month_end_arr_snapshot select row_number() over (order by period_month, segment), period_month, segment, active_subscriptions, ending_mrr, ending_arr, new_expansion_mrr, contraction_churn_mrr, refreshed_at from bv_mrr_movements;',
'create function fn_prorated_amount(monthly_amount numeric, active_days integer, days_in_period integer) returns numeric as select round(monthly_amount * active_days / nullif(days_in_period,0), 2);',
'create function fn_fiscal_period(calendar_date date) returns text as select strftime(''%Y'', calendar_date) || ''-P'' || printf(''%02d'', cast(strftime(''%m'', calendar_date) as integer));',
'finance_analytics', '2025-02-03 13:05:00', '2025-10-01 09:45:00', 'deployed', 'Fiscal-period helper used in board reporting extracts';
'select period_month, segment, sum(finance_arr) as finance_arr from mart_finance_arr_monthly group by period_month, segment order by period_month, segment;',
'finance_analytics', '2025-03-01 08:00:00', '2025-12-04 07:15:00', 'deployed', 'CSV extract for monthly board-pack ARR pages';
'select dunning_attempt_id, subscription_id, tenant_id, attempted_at, attempt_result from bill.dunning_attempt where attempt_result in (''failed'', ''final_notice'') order by attempted_at;',
'billing_ops', '2025-03-01 08:20:00', '2025-12-04 06:30:00', 'deployed', 'Daily dunning extract sent to billing operations';
'insert into workflow.mapping_request_case select * from workflow.mapping_request_case where status = ''open'';',
'revenue_operations', '2025-05-12 15:00:00', '2025-11-05 15:45:00', 'deployed', 'Workflow code object documenting SKU mapping exception case creation';

derivations.name: catalog_lineage_edge
sql: select 1, 'raw.usage_metering_event', 'stg.usage_metering_event', 'stg_usage_metering_event', 'deduplicate', 'latest ingested event_uid wins for replayed metering events', 0.98, '2025-12-31 04:15:00' union all
select 2, 'stg.usage_metering_event', 'bill.meter_reading', 'sp_rate_monthly_metered_billing', 'aggregate', 'monthly metered-unit rating by tenant and period', 0.97, '2025-12-31 04:15:00' union all
select 3, 'bill.invoice_line', 'wh.fact_invoice_line', 'wh_fact_invoice_line', 'load_fact', 'invoice line warehouse fact', 0.99, '2025-12-31 04:15:00' union all
select 4, 'wh.fact_invoice_line', 'nv_invoice_line', 'nv_invoice_line', 'view_join', 'invoice fact rejoined to tenant, plan, and date dimensions', 0.99, '2025-12-31 04:15:00' union all

select 5, 'wh.fact_subscription_month', 'nv_subscription_month', 'nv_subscription_month', 'view_join', 'subscription month fact rejoined to tenant, plan, and date dimensions', 0.99, '2025-12-31 04:15:00' union all
select 6, 'wh.fact_usage_daily', 'nv_usage_daily', 'nv_usage_daily', 'view_join', 'usage fact rejoined to tenant, feature, and date dimensions', 0.98, '2025-12-31 04:15:00' union all
select 7, 'nv_subscription_month', 'bv_mrr_movements', 'bv_mrr_movements', 'metric_logic', 'monthly MRR roll-forward and ARR movement calculations', 0.96, '2025-12-31 04:15:00' union all
select 8, 'bv_mrr_movements', 'mv.month_end_arr_snapshot', 'sp_rebuild_mv_month_end_arr_snapshot', 'materialize', 'month-end ARR snapshot refresh', 0.95, '2025-12-31 04:15:00' union all

select 9, 'manual.sku_revenue_line_mapping', 'mart_finance_arr_monthly', 'mart_finance_arr_monthly', 'manual_mapping', 'finance applies audited SKU-to-revenue-line mapping', 0.92, '2025-12-31 04:15:00' union all

select 10, 'mv.month_end_arr_snapshot', 'mart_customersuccess_arr_monthly', 'mart_customersuccess_arr_monthly', 'business_view', 'customer success uses subscription ARR without finance SKU mapping', 0.94, '2025-12-31 04:15:00' union all
select 11, 'mart_finance_arr_monthly', 'extract_board_pack_arr_monthly', 'extract_board_pack_arr_monthly', 'extract', 'board-pack ARR CSV extract', 0.91, '2025-12-31 04:15:00' union all
select 12, 'bill.dunning_attempt', 'extract_dunning_retry_queue', 'extract_dunning_retry_queue', 'extract', 'dunning queue extract for failed and final-notice attempts', 0.93, '2025-12-31 04:15:00';

imperfections.name: tenant_duplicate_signups
imperfections.scenario: Self-serve re-signups create near-duplicate tenants; same contact phone, fuzzed name. Surfaces in DQ-003 (no billing account) and the MDM merge queue.

imperfections.name: billing_account_late_sync
imperfections.scenario: Nightly app-to-billing sync misses runs; accounts land days late.

imperfections.name: ticket_requester_orphans
imperfections.scenario: Requesters hard-deleted from the IdP after offboarding -> DQ-002.

imperfections.name: usage_retired_feature_orphans
imperfections.scenario: Old SDK versions emit events for retired feature ids -> DQ-006; metering silently drops them.

imperfections.name: ticket_tenant_name_typos
imperfections.scenario: Agents hand-key tenant names on tickets; fuzzy matching required -> DQ-009.

imperfections.name: subscription_comp_overrides
imperfections.scenario: Sales comps subscriptions but the rating engine keeps billing full MRR -> DQ-011.

imperfections.name: webhook_retry_duplicates
imperfections.scenario: Processor retries deliver the same event_uid twice -> DQ-005 dedupe queue.

imperfections.name: missing_billing_xref
imperfections.scenario: Crosswalk rows lost during the 2024 billing migration -> DQ-003; core.tenant left with NULL billing_account_id.

imperfections.name: raw_billing_date_drift
imperfections.scenario: Legacy extract job pads dates after a patch; staging parses them to NULL -> DQ-004.

imperfections.name: stg_account_name_conflicts
imperfections.scenario: Staging survivorship mangles some names vs the billing source -> DQ-010.

imperfections.name: invoice_line_restatements
imperfections.scenario: Quarter-end billing corrections issued as reversal + restated pairs; these create the CTL-001 invoice-vs-lines recon breaks.

imperfections.name: stale_entitlement_mappings
imperfections.scenario: Entitlements expired but never revoked -> DQ-007, the access-review finding.

controls.rule_name: Invoice header total ties to invoice lines
Provisioned IdP users vs billed seats true-up
Finance ARR ties to customer-success ARR definition
controls.expected_break_rate: structural semantic variance from SKU mapping and metered ARR treatment

controls.rule_name: Metered usage staging ties to monthly rated billing
controls.expected_break_rate: raw replayed events visible but deduped before rating

dq_rules.condition: billing accounts have a billing email
ticket requester exists in the IdP
every tenant has an active billing mapping
event user belongs to the event tenant
free-text tenant name matches tenant master
invoice source SKUs have an active approved finance mapping
raw metering event_uid appears once after staging
invoice and due dates parse as MM/DD/YYYY

dataflows.name: tenant_mastering
dataflows.sequence: app.tenant + bill.customer_account -> raw extract -> staging -> xref -> core.tenant -> wh.dim_tenant

dataflows.name: trial_to_paid
dataflows.sequence: app.tenant signup -> bill.subscription (trialing) -> subscription_lifecycle machine -> activated_at -> bill.invoice

dataflows.name: meter_to_bill
dataflows.sequence: app.usage_event -> raw.usage_metering_event -> stg.usage_metering_event dedup -> bill.meter_reading -> overage lines on bill.invoice/invoice_line -> wh facts -> nv/bv/marts

dataflows.name: dunning
dataflows.sequence: subscription_lifecycle past_due/in_dunning -> bill.dunning_attempt (+3/+7/+14/+20d) -> recovered or cancelled_involuntary

dataflows.name: support
dataflows.sequence: sup.ticket -> ticket_triage machine -> raw.support_ticket_export -> stg.support_ticket -> wh.fact_support_ticket

dataflows.name: code_catalog_lineage
dataflows.sequence: sqlite_master views + stored routine/extract source -> catalog.code_object -> catalog.lineage_edge

dataflows.name: finance_vs_customer_success_arr
dataflows.sequence: bv_mrr_movements -> mv.month_end_arr_snapshot -> mart_customersuccess_arr_monthly; nv_invoice_line + manual mapping -> mart_finance_arr_monthly -> control_recon_arr_finance_vs_cs
